prevalence.trend: episodic outbreaks; urban transmission supported by Aedes aegypti

burden.mortalityRate: Low; chronic arthralgia contributes to disability

riskFactors: water storage and inadequate waste management
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute fever with severe polyarthralgia; confirmation by RT-PCR (early) or IgM; differentiate from dengue

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; outbreak-based surveillance and fever clinic testing in some states
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Acute recovery common; persistent joint pain in a subset with limited rehab access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common in outbreaks; lab testing concentrated in public health labs and tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care (paracetamol, hydration); avoid NSAIDs until dengue excluded; physiotherapy for persistent arthralgia
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underreporting, variable lab access, and limited chronic pain/rehab services for post-chikungunya arthritis
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established vector-borne disease control infrastructure and growing community awareness during outbreaks
